thesis: Analyst revisions are strongly positive and the stock is meaningfully cheaper than peers with firm momentum; a high-conviction Energy anchor with downside protection from low valuation.
falsification: Crude/refining margins roll over and revisions turn down, or ONGC underperforms NIFTY 500 over the hold while energy peers fall together.

thesis: Deep relative value plus strong analyst-revision support in refining; a value-with-a-catalyst Energy mid-cap where the revisions lead the still-lagging price.
falsification: Refining cracks compress and revisions fade, or the stock fails to re-rate within ~6 months despite cheapness.

thesis: Strong revisions plus confirming momentum in iron-ore/minerals; lifted to genuine conviction.
falsification: Iron-ore demand weakens, revisions roll over, or momentum reverses below peers.

thesis: Aluminium leader with confirming momentum and positive revisions at an attractive valuation; core Materials position.
falsification: Global aluminium/LME prices fall and revisions turn negative, or it lags the Materials block.

thesis: High momentum plus positive revisions in aluminium/alumina; a confirming satellite to Hindalco.
falsification: Momentum reverses sharply (commodity top) and revisions fade, or it underperforms Hindalco over the hold.

thesis: Zinc producer with firm momentum and supportive revisions; diversifies the base-metals sleeve.
falsification: Zinc prices fall, revisions turn down, or dividend-policy/parent overhang drags it below peers.

thesis: As above — defensive, cheap, high-yield Energy with positive revisions; carry plus ballast.
falsification: Coal volumes/realisations disappoint or a sharp renewables-driven de-rating.

thesis: Upstream oil with positive revisions and value support; second leg of the Energy thesis.
falsification: Crude falls and revisions roll over, or it simply tracks ONGC with no added breadth benefit.

thesis: Strong analyst-revision momentum on a diversified franchise (VSF, chemicals, cement, new-energy/paints optionality); a higher-quality Materials anchor.
falsification: Revisions reverse or the paints/new-business cash burn drags returns below peers over the hold.

thesis: Gold-loan NBFC with strong revisions, benefiting from elevated gold prices and steady AUM growth; a high-quality Financials addition.
falsification: Gold prices fall hard or regulatory tightening on gold-loan LTVs hits growth and revisions turn down.

thesis: Dominant life insurer with very strong rising revisions and modest valuation; an under-owned large-cap with re-rating optionality.
falsification: VNB/margin disappointment or persistent state-overhang discount keeps it cheap with revisions fading.

thesis: Private bank with improving revisions off a low base and attractive valuation; a mean-reversion Financials position.
falsification: Asset-quality or governance concerns re-emerge, revisions reverse, or it lags the bank index.

thesis: Steel producer with confirming momentum and value support; adds ferrous breadth alongside the non-ferrous cluster.
falsification: Steel prices/spreads fall and revisions turn down, or high operating leverage works against it in a downturn.

thesis: Injectables/CDMO name with strong revisions and confirming momentum; a quality Healthcare growth position.
falsification: US generic pricing pressure or a key approval slips and revisions roll over.

thesis: Diversified pharma with strong revisions and a reasonable valuation; second Healthcare leg for breadth.
falsification: US base-business erosion accelerates or pipeline disappoints and revisions fade.

thesis: High-growth IT services name with strong analyst-revision support and a robust deal pipeline; a quality offset to the cyclical tilt.
falsification: Deal-book or margin guidance is cut, revisions reverse, or discretionary IT spend weakens further.

thesis: Mid-cap IT with positive revisions and BFSI exposure turning; hold as IT-services representation.
falsification: BFSI tech spend stays weak, revisions turn down, or momentum keeps deteriorating.

thesis: Shipping name with strong momentum, supportive value and positive revisions; adds an uncorrelated Industrials cyclical.
falsification: Freight/tanker rates fall and revisions roll over, or the highly cyclical earnings peak and reverse.

thesis: Explosives and defence supplier with strong revisions and momentum riding a multi-year order cycle; a structural Materials/Industrials grower.
falsification: Order inflow slows, defence export momentum stalls, or the premium valuation de-rates as revisions fade.

thesis: High-quality staples franchise with improving revisions; provides low-beta defensive ballast to the cyclical tilt.
falsification: Volume growth stalls and revisions turn down, or rural demand stays weak.

thesis: Two-wheeler/export franchise with positive revisions and strong cash returns; the core Auto position.
falsification: Export/2W demand weakens, revisions reverse, or it lags the auto index.

thesis: Dominant paints franchise where revisions have turned strongly positive ahead of price; a quality recovery hold.
falsification: Competitive intensity (new entrant) keeps compressing margins and revisions reverse, or price momentum stays negative through the hold.

thesis: Siemens Energy India riding grid and power-equipment capex with strong revisions; a structural Industrials position.
falsification: Order momentum slows or the rich valuation de-rates as revisions fade.

thesis: Eyewear retail/omnichannel scaler with strong revisions; a high-growth Consumer addition sized modestly given valuation risk.
falsification: Growth/profitability disappoints post-listing and revisions reverse, or the lock-up/valuation overhang dominates.

thesis: Beauty/fashion e-commerce with strong revisions and improving profitability; small high-growth Consumer satellite.
falsification: Take-rate/contribution-margin progress stalls and revisions turn down.

thesis: Diversified metals/resources with strong momentum, value and positive revisions plus dividend carry; complements the base-metals cluster.
falsification: Commodity prices fall, the parent-debt/demerger overhang re-rates it down, or revisions reverse.

thesis: Small private bank with improving revisions and momentum off a low base; a tactical Financials recovery hold.
falsification: MFI asset-quality stress re-accelerates, revisions reverse, or momentum fades.

thesis: Regulated-return utility with positive revisions, value and yield; low-beta ballast and renewables optionality.
falsification: Capex/returns disappoint or revisions turn negative.

thesis: Gas/liquid-logistics terminal operator with strong revisions riding LPG/chemical throughput growth; an Industrials/Energy-infra satellite.
falsification: Throughput growth slows or revisions reverse.

thesis: Forging exporter with auto + defence + industrials diversification, positive momentum and revisions; adds Auto-ancillary breadth.
falsification: Global truck/auto demand weakens, defence ramp slips, and revisions reverse.

thesis: Specialty/branded pharma with positive momentum and revisions plus innovation (oncology) optionality; a Healthcare hold.
falsification: Pipeline/out-licensing newsflow disappoints and revisions turn down.

thesis: Very cheap PSU bank as a value/mean-reversion satellite; kept small precisely because revisions are not yet confirming.
falsification: Asset quality deteriorates, or revisions stay weak and the value gap persists through the hold.

thesis: Well-capitalised private bank at a reasonable valuation; a core large-cap Financials holding for index-relative balance.
falsification: NIM/credit-cost cycle turns and revisions fall below peers, or it lags the bank index.

thesis: Highest-quality large private bank — a low-tracking-error Financials anchor that limits benchmark-relative risk on a banks-heavy NIFTY 500.
falsification: Growth/asset-quality deteriorates and revisions turn negative, or it materially lags the bank index.

thesis: Largest PSU bank kept at a reduced weight purely to limit benchmark-relative risk, not on signal conviction.
falsification: Revisions and momentum turn convincingly positive (then I would add), or it underperforms private banks further (then exit).

thesis: Heavyweight conglomerate held near a benchmark stub to avoid a large active underweight on a flat signal; retail/Jio/new-energy optionality.
falsification: Revisions deteriorate further and it underperforms, in which case a deeper underweight is warranted.

thesis: Telecom with ARPU-led FCF improvement; a defensive large-cap held near benchmark weight.
falsification: ARPU/tariff momentum stalls and revisions fall, or it lags the index over the hold.

thesis: Largest pharma kept small for sector balance; not a conviction position given flat revisions.
falsification: Specialty pipeline accelerates and revisions turn up (then add), or it underperforms pharma peers (then exit).
